title: Biometric electronic signature tokens
one_sentence_summary: A system generates biometric electronic signature tokens by tokenizing a user's biometric sample, hashing it, binding it to a timestamp, and digitally signing the result with a private key to provide two-factor authentication.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method of generating a biometric electronic signature token by tokenizing a biometric sample, cryptographically binding its hash to a trusted time stamp, and digitally signing both the record and the tokenized sample with a user's private key. This process enables dual-factor authentication combining possession of a private key and inherence of a biometric trait.
independent_claim_subjects: method for generating biometric electronic signature token; system for managing biometric electronic signature token; method for verifying biometric electronic signature token

title: Method of creating and recovering digital wallet
one_sentence_summary: A method for creating and recovering a digital wallet using a closed storage device with a security element and a connected device, where a recovery mnemonic is displayed on the storage device for manual backup to prevent unauthorized access.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method where a recovery mnemonic is generated by a security element and displayed directly on a closed storage device's information conveying unit, requiring the user to manually record it before the connected device can access the digital wallet. This ensures that even if the connected device is compromised, the wallet cannot be recovered without physical access to the storage device and the manually recorded mnemonic.
independent_claim_subjects: method of creating and recovering digital wallet via closed storage device; method of creating and recovering digital wallet via first and second connected devices

title: Methods and systems for crowdsourcing an outcome to an issue
one_sentence_summary: A blockchain-based voting system assigns hashed identifiers and public keys to users, digitally signs votes, and immutably stores records on a distributed ledger to ensure verifiable and auditable election outcomes.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method for crowdsourcing election outcomes by generating immutable blockchain records containing a user's hashed identifier, public key, and voting data. It further enables users to revise votes prior to an election closing date by generating new signed records that replace or update the previous vote on the distributed ledger.

title: Method and apparatus for providing secure communication among constrained devices
one_sentence_summary: A method for managing cryptographic updates in constrained IoT devices by identifying communication partners via certificates and synchronizing algorithm replacements to maintain compatibility.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention issues cryptographic communication rights (certificates or tickets) to constrained devices that store only one algorithm per function. It identifies a subset of devices that must share the same algorithm to communicate and coordinates a simultaneous update of their cryptographic code modules to prevent communication breaks.

title: User authentication with self-signed certificate and identity verification and migration
one_sentence_summary: A user authentication system replaces passwords with self-signed certificate chains, allowing users to act as their own Certificate Authority and migrate trust between devices via secure enclaves.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention enables users to generate a self-signed root certificate and intermediate certificates signed by that root to form a chain of trust. This chain is used to authenticate the user to a verifying party and can be migrated to new devices by signing new intermediate certificates with the root key.

title: Trusted execution environment secure element communication
one_sentence_summary: A method for secure communication binds an isolated environment to a secure component, performing tamper checks via trusted boot attestation before acting as an encrypted intermediary for data exchange.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention establishes a secure communication channel between an isolated environment (TEE) and a secure component (SE) by binding them via encrypted channels. It utilizes the isolated environment to perform tamper checks, including trusted boot attestation and device root key verification, before acting as an intermediary to decrypt and forward data to normal world applications.

title: Architecture and methods for self-sovereign digital identity
one_sentence_summary: A self-sovereign digital identity architecture using identity engines on personal devices to manage keys, verify possession and custody, and exchange identities via adapted Diffie-Hellman protocols.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method for synchronously verifying digital identities by combining proof-of-possession via challenge-response with proof-of-custody via user authentication. It further claims a method for securely exchanging identities using an adapted Diffie-Hellman key exchange to derive symmetric keys for encrypting the transfer of digital identity artifacts.

title: Cryptographic methods and systems for managing digital certificates with linkage values
inventors: Simplicio, Jr.; Marcos A. et al.
assignee: LG Electronics, Inc.,University of Sao Paulo
one_sentence_summary: A method for managing digital certificates in connected vehicles using split linkage values to enable group revocation while eliminating the need for a dedicated linkage authority.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention distributes the generation of linkage values between two entities, where one entity combines its pre-linkage value with an encrypted pre-linkage value from a second entity without decryption. This allows the second entity to create certificates with linkage values that enable group revocation without either entity knowing the full linkage value or being able to track users alone.

one_sentence_summary: A method for simplifying account registration and authentication across multiple applications using PKI, secure elements, and blockchain to verify user identity via encrypted variable authentication information.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention streamlines account registration by leveraging an existing PKI-based account in a first application to authenticate a user for a second application. title: Key attestation statement generation providing device anonymity
one_sentence_summary: An attestation service issues certificates using a shared pool of reusable key pairs to provide device anonymity while encrypting private keys to trusted secure components.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention selects a single asymmetric key pair from a collection to be shared among multiple computing devices, generating distinct attestation certificates for each device that associate the shared public key with the device's trusted secure component. This approach prevents relying parties from colluding to identify specific devices because the same public key is used by multiple distinct entities.

title: Authentication and key agreement in communication network
one_sentence_summary: A method for authentication and key agreement in communication networks using group signatures for privacy-preserving identity verification and Diffie-Hellman key exchange for session key generation.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a network node to generate a common public key and master secret key, assigning pseudonym identifications and specific private keys to user equipment. It enables mutual authentication via group signatures and session key derivation using intermediate keys generated by the network node based on shared secrets and the common public key.

title: Incorporating risk-based decision in standard authentication and authorization systems
one_sentence_summary: A risk-based authentication system captures user and device attributes to dynamically determine security risk, granting access or requiring step-up authentication based on acceptable risk thresholds.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention integrates risk-based decision making into standard authentication flows by capturing live user and device attributes to compute a risk score. If the risk is acceptable, access is granted or tokens refreshed without additional authentication; if unacceptable, step-up authentication is required.

title: Backup system with multiple recovery keys
one_sentence_summary: A backup system encrypts data with a master key, which is encrypted by device public keys, while device private keys are escrowed on secure servers encrypted by user passcodes for recovery.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method where backup data is encrypted with a master recovery key, and copies of this key are encrypted with public keys of related devices. title: Apparatus and method for certificate enrollment
one_sentence_summary: An apparatus in a secure execution environment recovers cryptographic keys from a shared secret and certificate diversifiers to perform operations without storing private keys in clear text.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention enables dynamic key recovery within a secure execution environment by deriving cryptographic keys from a pre-seeded shared secret value and public diversifier values contained in a digital certificate. title: Method and apparatus for transmitting and receiving encrypted message between terminals
one_sentence_summary: A method for transmitting encrypted messages between terminals using a secure keyboard mode that generates encrypted text containing session keys wrapped with public keys of both sender and receiver.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a secure keyboard interface that encrypts user input into a character string containing a session key encrypted with both the sender's and receiver's public keys. one_sentence_summary: A communication server system secures message transmission by encrypting content with random symmetric keys and wrapping those keys with public keys of telecom servers or user devices, ensuring end-to-end privacy.

title: Systems and methods for blockchain-based digital lottery ticket generation and distribution
one_sentence_summary: A blockchain-based system generates and transfers digital lottery tickets using public key cryptography and a transaction certification authority to maintain an immutable, publicly viewable ledger.

title: Communication system, hardware security module, terminal device, communication method, and program
one_sentence_summary: A communication system where terminal devices generate unique CA key pairs and request certificates from a root CA to limit the impact of compromised secure elements on the overall PKI trust chain.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a system where each terminal device generates its own unique certification authority key pair and obtains a certificate for it from a root certification authority. This ensures that if one device's CA secret key is compromised, the validity of certificates issued by other devices remains intact, unlike systems sharing a common CA key.

title: Secure directory services
one_sentence_summary: A secure directory service enables contact discovery by matching hashed foreign identifiers against stored entries without revealing plaintext contact information to the server.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention discloses a method where a client device transforms foreign identifiers (like phone numbers) into cryptographic hashes before transmitting them to a server. title: Systems and methods for post-quantum cryptography communications channels
one_sentence_summary: A system splits electronic communications into overhead and payload portions, transmitting the former over a classical channel and the latter over a post-quantum cryptography channel via an add-on device.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a dual-channel architecture where classical circuitry transmits message overhead to a non-PQC client device while PQC callback circuitry transmits payload data or cryptographic keys to a coupled PQC add-on device. This separation allows legacy systems to migrate to quantum-resistant security by offloading sensitive data transmission to a dedicated PQC channel.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is discussed extensively in the Background and Detailed Description as the threat driver (Shor's algorithm, Grover's algorithm) and motivation for PQC. However, no independent or dependent claims require actual quantum hardware (QKD, QRNG, entanglement). The invention relies on classical 'PQC' algorithms resistant to quantum attacks, not quantum communication channels themselves.

title: Systems and methods for quantum consensus
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods for quantum entanglement authentication generate session keys by measuring subsets of entangled quantum particles to secure communications between computing devices.

title: Post-quantum cryptography side chain
one_sentence_summary: A method for securing blockchain data by encrypting blocks with classical techniques on a first side chain and re-encrypting them with post-quantum cryptography on a second side chain to mitigate future quantum threats.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention discloses a ledger system where blocks are encrypted using a first cryptographic technique and written to a first side chain, then accessed again to be encrypted with a second cryptographic technique (specifically post-quantum encryption) and written to a second side chain. This dual-layer approach allows for the migration of data security from classical to quantum-resistant algorithms without disrupting the primary blockchain.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned in the background as a threat (Shor's algorithm, Grover's algorithm) and as the motivation for using post-quantum cryptography. However, the independent claims (1, 10, 19) define the invention using generic 'first' and 'second' cryptographic techniques. The specific requirement that the second technique be 'post quantum encryption' appears only in dependent claims 2, 11, and 20. Therefore, the primary invention is not strictly limited to quantum resistance in its broadest scope, though it is a key embodiment.

one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods for quantum entanglement authentication generate session keys by measuring entangled particles at separate devices to verify identity without transmitting secret keys.

title: Key exchange schemes with addressable elements
assignee: Arizona Board of Regents on Behalf of Northern Arizona University,Government of the United States of America, as represented by the Secretary of the Air Force
one_sentence_summary: A computing device uses an array of addressable physical unclonable functions to generate private keys from public keys, enabling secure key exchange without storing static secrets in non-volatile memory.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes an array of addressable hardware elements, specifically Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs), to dynamically generate private keys based on addresses derived from a public key. quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned in the background section as a threat to current PKIs (RSA, ECC) and as a comparison point (QKD), but it is not claimed in any independent or dependent claims. The invention relies on classical PUF hardware, not quantum mechanics.

one_sentence_summary: A method for managing cryptographic algorithm transitions in a blockchain by generating digitally signed status records on a separate out-of-band blockchain to track active algorithms per block.

title: Methods for protecting data
one_sentence_summary: A method protects sensitive production data by decrypting it, applying homomorphic encryption and masking in a secure middle environment, then storing the masked cleartext in a lower security environment for testing.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a three-tier architecture where a secure middle environment acts as a proxy to transform production data. title: Cryptographic machines characterized by a Finite Lab-Transform (FLT)
one_sentence_summary: A cryptographic device modifies standard algorithms using a Finite Lab Transform on n-state switching tables with n greater than 4 to enhance security against cryptanalysis.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention applies a Finite Lab Transform (FLT) to standard n-state computer operations (n>4) by inserting reversible inverters on inputs and a reversing inverter on the output. This transforms standard operations like modulo-n multiplication or GF(n) arithmetic into non-standard switching tables that are not equivalent to standard finite field operations.

title: Systems and methods for quantum one-time pad generation
inventors: Carter, Jr.; Robert L. et al.
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods generate quantum one-time pads using entangled particles stored in separate devices to derive identical random numbers for session authentication and key generation without transmitting the keys.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention generates a first quantum one-time pad comprising a set of entangled quantum particles and stores them in quantum storage cells. quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 18, 19), which require generating and storing entangled quantum particles in quantum storage cells. title: Systems and methods for post-quantum cryptography communications channels
one_sentence_summary: A system transmits communication overhead over a classical channel while sending payload data encrypted with quantum keys over a separate post-quantum channel to mitigate quantum threats.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a communications system that splits a financial or healthcare message into overhead metadata (transmitted over a classical channel) and encrypted payload data (transmitted over a separate post-quantum cryptography channel). Quantum-generated session keys bind the two channels such that an adversary intercepting classical traffic cannot reconstruct the full message without also defeating the post-quantum protected payload, providing a migration path to quantum-safe communications while preserving compatibility with existing infrastructure.

title: Quantum phenomenon-based obfuscation of memory
one_sentence_summary: A method for securing data by disassembling it into sections and storing them at memory locations determined by a quantum obfuscation map derived from quantum particle states.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention discloses a method where data is disassembled into sections and stored in memory locations assigned via a Quantum Obfuscation Map (QOM). The QOM is generated based on Quantum Obfuscation Elements (QOEs) corresponding to the quantum state of a particle, such as a collapsed wave function or entangled pair.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 12, 20). The invention requires a Quantum Obfuscation Map (QOM) generated from Quantum Obfuscation Elements (QOEs) corresponding to a quantum state of a particle. Dependent claims specify QOEs as collapsed wave functions (claim 5) or derived from entangled particles (claim 11). The primary invention relies on these quantum states to determine memory addresses.
independent_claim_subjects: method for storing information using quantum phenomenon-based obfuscation; computing device comprising processing circuitry and cryptographic circuitry; computer program product for storing information using quantum phenomenon-based obfuscation

title: Cybersecurity infection detection system and method
one_sentence_summary: A cybersecurity infection detection system using redundant self-monitoring probes and monitors that self-destruct upon compromise, utilizing digital signatures, hashing, and encrypted communication channels to ensure defense in depth.

title: Systems and methods for quantum entangled random key exchange
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods for secure key exchange across intermediary devices using keys derived from random bit sets, optionally generated via quantum entanglement, to enable end-to-end encryption without exposing keys to intermediaries.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method where a first device derives distinct keys with a second and third device using separate random bit sets. It receives an encrypted message containing a common key from the second device, decrypts it, re-encrypts it with the key shared with the third device, and transmits it to establish secure communication.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology (QERNG) is mentioned in the title, abstract, and description as a source for random bit sets. However, it appears only in dependent claims 4, 5, 11, and 12. title: Systems and methods for mobile quantum key distribution
one_sentence_summary: A mobile quantum key distribution system uses a portable device to generate entangled particles and inject them into remote devices or derive secure keys for legacy hardware to facilitate secure communication.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention discloses a mobile quantum key distribution (MQKD) device that physically transports between locations to inject entangled particles directly into quantum-enabled devices. quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 9, 18). The invention requires generating a pair of entangled particles and injecting quantum data based on these particles. Thus, quantum generation is required for the core invention, though the transmission of raw quantum states is conditional on device capability.

title: Systems and methods for classical-quantum encryption and decryption
one_sentence_summary: A classical-quantum encryption system encodes plaintext into analytic function coefficients via Taylor series on a classical computer, requiring a quantum computer to reverse the process for decryption.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention creates an asymmetric paradigm where encryption is performed classically by generating a Taylor series expansion of an analytic function derived from a symmetric key and plaintext. Decryption is strictly dependent on a quantum computer to derive the original analytic function from the ciphertext coefficients, leveraging the computational difficulty of inverting Taylor series expansions.
quantum_notes: Quantum computing is central to the invention's decryption phase. While independent claims (1, 6, 11) describe the encryption method/apparatus/product which are classical, the Abstract and Description explicitly define the 'classical-quantum' paradigm where decryption requires a quantum computer to derive the analytic function from the Taylor series coefficients. The text states: 'decryption is performed using a quantum computer' and describes using quantum graph similarity and phase estimation algorithms for this purpose.
independent_claim_subjects: method for classical-quantum encryption; apparatus for classical-quantum encryption; computer program product for classical-quantum encryption

title: Systems and methods for establishing cryptographic keys shared among three or more devices
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods establish shared cryptographic keys among three or more devices using N-way entangled particle triplets to enable symmetric authentication and secure failover in distributed networks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method for establishing cryptographic keys shared among three or more devices by generating entangled particle triplets, distributing them to respective host devices, and verifying matching bit sets derived from quantum measurements. quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 15, 18). Claim 1 explicitly requires receiving bits derived from entangled particles. title: Key establishment and secure communications using satellite-provided random number values
one_sentence_summary: A system generates cryptographic keys by combining random values from quantum random number generators located on satellites and terrestrial stations, coordinated via key generation parameters to establish secure communication channels.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method where a terrestrial computing system receives first and second random values generated by quantum random number generators, with at least one value provided via satellite communication. These values are combined according to specific key generation parameters to serve as a seed for a cryptographic function that produces a shared symmetric key.
quantum_notes: Quantum Random Number Generators (QRNGs) are explicitly required in independent claims 1 and 12 as the source of the random values used for key generation. The invention relies on quantum-derived entropy provided via satellite or terrestrial QRNGs to ensure high-quality randomness.

title: BLOCKCHAIN USER ACCOUNT DATA
one_sentence_summary: A method for creating user accounts in a blockchain ledger by receiving an operation instruction with a user identifier and public key parameter, obtaining the public key, and writing the instruction and key into the ledger.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a centralized method for creating user accounts in a blockchain ledger where the server obtains a public key based on a user identifier and writes both the creation instruction and the public key into the ledger as an immutable data record. title: ONE-TIME PASSWORD WITH UNPREDICTABLE MOVING FACTOR
one_sentence_summary: A method for generating one-time passwords using a shared secret key and a current blockchain hash value as an unpredictable moving factor to prevent replay attacks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a current blockchain hash value as an unpredictable moving factor in a one-time password generation algorithm. This replaces predictable time-based or counter-based factors, rendering tokens obsolete immediately after use as the blockchain updates.

one_sentence_summary: A system distributes quantum random numbers from a QRNG to client devices via secure Software Defined Perimeter connections to enhance entropy for certificate generation and secure tunnel establishment.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention establishes a secure Software Defined Perimeter (SDP) channel to distribute a stream of quantum random numbers from a QRNG to a requesting client device. This distribution enables the client to generate high-entropy public key certificates and secure communication tunnels while preventing entropy exhaustion attacks.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 12, 20), which explicitly require obtaining a stream of quantum random numbers from a quantum random number generator (QRNG). Dependent claims (10, 18) further specify distribution via Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) links. The primary invention relies on the QRNG as the entropy source.

title: APPARATUS AND METHODS FOR BLOCKCHAIN-BASED VERIFICATION
one_sentence_summary: Apparatus and methods for verifying content distribution events like alternate content switching and ad display using blockchain hashes to ensure transparency and privacy without transmitting voluminous raw data.

title: MAINTAINING TRUST ON A DATA STORAGE NETWORK

title: BLIND KEY GENERATOR AND EXCHANGE
one_sentence_summary: A blind key exchange system uses physically unclonable functions and class functions to derive final encryption keys without transmitting them, securing IoT communications.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention derives a final encryption key using a class function applied to a partial key generated from a physically unclonable function (PUF) and an initialization vector. This method ensures the final key is never transmitted, only a partial key that bears no resemblance to the final key is sent to the recipient.

title: DATA PROTECTION IN A TRACKING DEVICE ENVIRONMENT
one_sentence_summary: A tracking system uses permanent and temporary asymmetric key pairs to enable community devices to encrypt location data that only the owner can decrypt, ensuring end-to-end privacy.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention generates a permanent encryption key pair for a tracking device and a set of temporary encryption key pairs. title: TRUSTED EXECUTION ENVIRONMENT VERIFICATION OF A SOFTWARE PACKAGE
one_sentence_summary: A method verifies software package integrity within a Trusted Execution Environment by comparing calculated cryptographic hashes and validating digital signatures before installation.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention initiates a software package verification process within a hardware-implemented Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) isolated from the host OS. title: CRYPTOGRAPHIC FEATURE LICENSING
one_sentence_summary: A method for secure feature licensing of industrial devices using a hardware root of trust to store unique private keys, enabling device-specific encryption and certificate authority-signed validation of license packages.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR VERIFYING INTEGRITY OF GPS POSITION INFORMATION
assignee: INDUSTRY ACADEMY COOPERATION FOUNDATION OF SEJONG UNIVERSITY
one_sentence_summary: A method for unmanned moving objects to verify GPS integrity by authenticating base stations via digital signatures and comparing GPS-derived dynamics with position measurements from authenticated terrestrial base stations.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method where an unmanned moving object authenticates connected base stations using a signature scheme involving nonces and secret keys derived from an ID. It then verifies GPS integrity by calculating and comparing the dynamics (speed and direction) derived from the authenticated base station measurements against the dynamics derived from the received GPS signals.

title: SECURE STORAGE OF PASSWORDS
one_sentence_summary: A system secures password storage by using a Hardware Security Module to generate and verify Message Authentication Codes based on plaintext passwords and salts, preventing database theft from revealing credentials.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a Hardware Security Module (HSM) to generate and verify Message Authentication Codes (MACs) using a secret key stored exclusively within the HSM. This approach ensures that even if the database containing MACs and salts is stolen, the plaintext passwords cannot be derived without access to the HSM's internal secret key.

title: SECURE MULTI-PARTY RANDOM BIT GENERATION
one_sentence_summary: A secure multi-party random bit generation protocol using broadcast protocols and trusted execution environments to ensure randomness even with malicious participants.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes secure environments like trusted execution environments to store private protocol instance keys and employs broadcast protocols such as Dolev-Strong to distribute public keys. title: An ECDHE Key Exchange for Server Authentication and a Key Server
one_sentence_summary: A method for authenticated ECDHE key exchange using a key server to derive a shared secret via ECC point addition of two separate ECDH exchanges, enabling server authentication without full certificate chain verification.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a three-party architecture where a key server performs an ECDH exchange with a device's ephemeral public key and a static network private key to derive a first secret. title: Network Coding-Based Post-Quantum Cryptography
one_sentence_summary: A hybrid communication scheme mixes data using linear network coding and encrypts only a subset of encoded messages with post-quantum cryptography to achieve high rates and computational security.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention combines linear network coding with selective post-quantum encryption, where messages are pre-mixed so that unencrypted portions remain secure against eavesdroppers who cannot decrypt the encrypted subset. This allows the communication rate to approach one as the number of channels increases while maintaining post-quantum security guarantees.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned in the background regarding Shor's algorithm breaking RSA and in the description of post-quantum security. Claims 5 and 13 specifically recite 'post-quantum encryption' as an optional embodiment of the encryption step. The primary invention (Claims 1 and 9) does not strictly require post-quantum encryption, as it can utilize any public-key encryption (Claim 4/12), making quantum resistance an optional feature rather than a core requirement of the independent claims.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR FEDERATED LEARNING USING DISTRIBUTED MESSAGING WITH ENTITLEMENTS FOR ANONYMOUS COMPUTATION AND SECURE DELIVERY OF MODEL

primary_inventive_claim: The invention employs a specific distributed messaging topology with distinct plain and encrypted channels to facilitate anonymous submission of local ML models. title: Systems and Methods for Hiding Private Cryptographic Keys in Multimedia Files
one_sentence_summary: A system and method for hiding private cryptographic keys, including post-quantum keys, within multimedia files using steganography based on Goppa codes to secure data against quantum computer attacks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method for hiding private data in operable multimedia files using steganography implemented via error-correction determined by a Goppa code. It specifically enables the concealment of post-quantum cryptography secret keys within standard media formats while maintaining the appearance of unaltered data to the user.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned in the abstract and background as the threat model (quantum computers breaking RSA). The primary invention (claims 1, 7, 13) is a steganographic method using Goppa codes. Quantum relevance appears only in dependent claims 6, 12, and 19, which specify that the hidden private data can be a 'post-quantum cryptography (PQC) secret key'. The core steganographic mechanism does not require quantum technology to function, but is designed to protect keys that are quantum-resistant.
independent_claim_subjects: method for hiding private data in operable data; cryptographic system for hiding private data in multimedia files; method for hiding private data in multimedia files using PIN

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR ESTABLISHING AND MAINTAINING TRUST FOR SECURE COMMUNICATIONS
one_sentence_summary: A trust framework establishes secure communications between clients and hosts via anchors using sustained bond states containing hash codes of public keys, eliminating reliance on third-party certificate authorities.

title: TECHNOLOGIES FOR USING NFC OR QR CODE TO COMMISSION A DEVICE TO THE CLOUD
one_sentence_summary: A method for commissioning offline access control devices to a cloud system using NFC or QR codes to retrieve encrypted commissioning data secured by factory-generated cryptographic key pairs.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD WITH HOMOMORPHIC ENCRYPTION
assignee: Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.,Seoul National University R&DB Foundation,Industry Academic Cooperation Foundation, Chosun University,Daegu Gyeongbuk Institute of Science and Technology
one_sentence_summary: An apparatus and method for performing homomorphic encryption operations on encrypted images by packing data into extended tensors to maintain computational efficiency during convolution with strides greater than one.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method of generating packed data by mapping an encrypted image to an extended tensor based on a mapping constant derived from tensor dimensions. This packed data is then used in a homomorphic convolution operation, followed by rotation and addition to extract valid values, thereby preventing data density loss associated with strided convolutions.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned only in the Background section (Description) stating that homomorphic encryption is 'lattice-based and thus, resistant to quantum algorithms'. No independent or dependent claims require or describe quantum technology; the invention is a classical optimization for homomorphic encryption.
independent_claim_subjects: An apparatus comprising processors configured to generate packed data and perform homomorphic encryption; A processor-implemented method comprising generating packed data and performing homomorphic encryption; A processor-implemented method comprising determining a mapping constant and performing a convolution operation

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM OF ENCODING DATA OVER DISTRIBUTED NETWORKS AND METHOD OF ASSURING INTEGRITY OF DATA TRANSMISSION BETWEEN SENDER AND RECEIVER IN A COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
one_sentence_summary: A method for encoding data into holographic tokens using fractionalization, encryption, polar coding, and superimposed coding with entanglement to ensure integrity and anti-fragility over distributed networks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method of encoding data by fractionalizing it into shards, encrypting them, applying polar coding to generate polarized tokens, and dispersing them via superimposed coding to create holographic tokens. These tokens are further intermixed via entanglement to homogenize identities, ensuring the data cannot be cloned and remains resilient against network catastrophes.
quantum_notes: Quantum concepts, specifically 'entanglement' and 'superpositioning', are central to the independent claims (1, 12, 21). Claim 1 requires dispersing tokens using superimposed coding (superpositioning). Claim 5 (dependent on 2) and Claim 15 (dependent on 13) explicitly require intermixing tokens via entanglement to homogenize identities. Claim 21 (independent) explicitly includes intermixing via entanglement as a step in the method. The invention relies on these quantum-inspired principles for its claimed anti-fragility and non-clonability.
independent_claim_subjects: method for encoding data over distributed networks; computer system for encoding data; method of assuring integrity of data transmission

title: PUBLIC KEY AUTHENTICATION DEVICE AND PUBLIC KEY AUTHENTICATION METHOD
one_sentence_summary: A public key authentication device for SIP telephones uses an IC card to securely store keys and signs a URI and a server-generated random number to prevent spoofing attacks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention authenticates a SIP calling party by having a PKI server generate a random number and the caller sign both the random number and their URI using a private key stored in a secure IC card. title: Transitioning To and From Crypto-Agile Hybrid Public Key Infrastructures
one_sentence_summary: A method for transitioning a PKI system to a new cryptosystem by inserting a secondary crypto-agile hybrid root certificate containing both old and new public keys into the certificate chain.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention generates a secondary crypto-agile root certificate containing a first public key from an old cryptosystem and a second public key from a new cryptosystem, signed by both corresponding private keys. title: Enhanced Security for Existing Public Key Infrastructure
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods convert existing CPA-secure public key infrastructure into CCA-secure encryption using hinting pseudorandom generators and equivocal commitments without requiring new key distribution.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method to adapt a chosen plaintext attack (CPA) secure encryption scheme into a chosen ciphertext attack (CCA) secure scheme by utilizing a hinting pseudorandom generator and equivocal commitments. title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR IDENTITY MANAGEMENT
one_sentence_summary: A system and method for PKI-enabled SIM digital identity management involving document verification, CSR generation within the SIM, and secure transfer of digital identities via middleware.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention integrates Public Key Infrastructure directly into a Subscriber Information Management (SIM) card to generate and store digital identities. title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR ENHANCED DIGITAL SIGNATURE SCHEMES
one_sentence_summary: A system for enhanced digital signatures in PKI using an improved McEliece code-based scheme with Goppa codes to sign certificates while reducing processing and storage requirements.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a digital signature scheme for PKI certificates based on an enhanced McEliece cryptosystem using Goppa codes. It utilizes a random vector derived from a seed to concatenate with a certificate hash, computing a syndrome and codeword to generate a signature that avoids the security and performance limitations of conventional schemes like MGD.
quantum_notes: Quantum computing is mentioned in the Background section as a threat driving the need for post-quantum alternatives, but no quantum technology (QKD, QRNG, etc.) is claimed or required for the invention. The invention itself is a classical post-quantum algorithm (code-based).

title: Temporary Enablement of Functionality of a Secure Device

primary_inventive_claim: The invention enables temporary functionality on a secure device without storing reusable credentials by generating a transient HMAC key and device-unique data to create a challenge. A trusted device signs this challenge, and the secure device verifies the signature using a stored public key to authorize the temporary enablement.

one_sentence_summary: A system for secure machine learning inference using a trusted processing circuit, public key verification against a database, blind signatures for input data, and usage limits embedded in encrypted models.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a system where a trusted processing circuit decrypts an encrypted machine learning model only after its public key is verified against a database and a zero-knowledge proof confirms possession of the private key. It further secures inference by enforcing usage limits and requiring blind signatures on input data.

title: Agile Machine Cryptography Based On Real-Time Computer Function Transformation
one_sentence_summary: A cryptographic method transforms computer functions using n-state reversible inverters derived from PKI keys to dynamically modify encryption, hashing, and signature operations.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention generates an n-state reversible inverter from a sequence of bits (potentially a PKI key) to transform standard computer functions like addition or XOR. This transformation creates a modified cryptographic operation that is self-propagating and changes the underlying logic structure of the cipher or hash function.

title: COMMUNICATION METHOD AND APPARATUS
one_sentence_summary: A communication method prevents network function impersonation during certificate requests by requiring a trusted function signature verifying the instance identity before the CA issues a certificate.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention introduces a verification mechanism where a Certificate Authority validates a signature from a trusted function (e.g., OAM) covering a second instance identity against a first instance identity in a certificate request. This ensures the requesting Network Function is not impersonating another entity before issuing a certificate.

title: SERIAL NUMBER GENERATION FOR STATELESS CLOUD CERTIFICATE AUTHORITY
one_sentence_summary: A stateless cloud certificate authority system generates unique X.509 serial numbers deterministically by concatenating instance index, deployment time, generation timestamp, and a counter to avoid database lookups.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a deterministic algorithm for generating unique certificate serial numbers in a stateless cloud environment without relying on random number generators or database checks. It achieves uniqueness by concatenating an instance index, instance deployment time, a monotonically increasing generation timestamp, and a counter managed via a mutex.

title: Hybrid AI Failover and Secure Field-Data Capture System with Shadow-Mode Validation and Training-to-Inference Reassignment
one_sentence_summary: A hybrid AI failover system executes primary and candidate models in parallel with shadow-mode validation to ensure output alignment before transferring inference authority in safety-critical environments.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a system where a candidate AI model runs in shadow mode alongside a primary model, comparing outputs via a divergence analysis module. Inference authority is transferred only when alignment criteria are met or upon failure detection, ensuring controlled transitions to reduce system instability.

title: PKI SMART-CARD THREAT DETECTION AND ENDPOINT USE VISIBILITY
one_sentence_summary: A system using middleware on endpoints to generate and upload events regarding PKI authenticator device usage, enabling server-side analytics to detect anomalies, unauthorized applications, and potential attacks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a middleware layer that intercepts API calls between applications and PKI authenticator devices to generate detailed events. These events are uploaded to a server where an analytics engine correlates application identity with device usage patterns to identify anomalies and potential threats like keyloggers or unauthorized access.
